title: South Korea to Lead RIMPAC Maritime Exercise for First Time
summary: South Korea will command multinational naval forces in the US-led RIMPAC exercise scheduled for June 24 to July 31, marking the first time the country has held the lead role.
contents:
# South Korea to Lead RIMPAC Maritime Exercise for First Time

## Overview
South Korea will command multinational naval forces in the US-led RIMPAC exercise scheduled for June 24 to July 31, marking the first time the country has held the lead role.

## Details
A South Korean admiral will lead over 25,000 personnel, approximately 40 surface ships, five submarines, and 140 aircraft in the biennial drill. South Korea has participated in RIMPAC since 1990 and served as deputy commander in 2024. The country will deploy its Aegis destroyer Jeongjo the Great, P-9 maritime patrol aircraft, and the 3,000-ton Dosan Ahn Chang-ho submarine. This exercise represents a significant expansion of South Korea's role in regional maritime security operations.

## Location
Hawaii, United States

## Source Context
Reporting referenced from coverage tracked by the @TheKorea_Times OSINT beat. The cited outlet provides additional context, attribution, and verification of the claims summarized above.

## Tags
South Korea, RIMPAC, maritime exercise, naval forces, US-Korea alliance, regional security, multinational drill, Indo-Pacific
